Typed-handler misuse: wrong signatures, plain-handler shape, generics.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/web v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/web => WEBDIR
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- web/web.go --
package web

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/web"
)

//fabrik:web GET /a
func PlainShape(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}

//fabrik:web GET /b
func NoError(req *web.Request) web.Response { return nil }

//fabrik:web GET /c
func WrongParam(s string) (web.Response, error) { return nil, nil }

//fabrik:web GET /d
func Generic[T any](req *web.Request) (web.Response, error) { return nil, nil }
-- want/diags --
error: web/web.go:9:1: handler PlainShape has the wrong signature
  help: want func(req *web.Request) (web.Response, error)
error: web/web.go:12:1: handler NoError has the wrong signature
  help: want func(req *web.Request) (web.Response, error)
error: web/web.go:15:1: handler WrongParam has the wrong signature
  help: want func(req *web.Request) (web.Response, error)
error: web/web.go:18:1: handler Generic cannot be generic (generated code cannot instantiate type parameters)
  help: declare a concrete handler
